name: Léognan and La Brède Forest Loop
name_original: Boucle à partir de Villenave d'Ornon
description: This moderate 28.5-mile mountain biking loop from Villenave d'Ornon offers varied terrain, forest sections, and singletrack.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Villenave-D'Ornon offers a diverse landscape of urban green spaces, tranquil lake-side paths, and accessible forest trails. The region features numerous parks like Parc de Mussonville and Parc de l'Ermitage, providing winding paths suitable for cycling. Versein Lake offers well-maintained paths along its edge, and nearby forests such as Canéjan Forest extend the options for off-road experiences. The terrain is generally flat, with gentle elevation changes, making it suitable for various skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Villenave-D'Ornon The most popular mountain bike trail is Léognan and La Brède Forest Loop, a 28.6 miles (45.9 km) moderate route that takes about 3 hours 3 minutes to complete, winding through extensive forest areas. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Cayac Priory – Moulin de Rouillac loop from Villenave d'Ornon, a moderate 28.1 miles (45.3 km) path that explores a mix of green spaces and historical sites. Local mountain bikers also love the Moulin de Rouillac – Green Lake loop from Villenave d'Ornon, a 22.5 miles (36.2 km) trail leading through varied landscapes including lake areas, often completed in about 2 hours 34 minutes. Mountain biking around Villenave-D'Ornon is defined by its numerous parks, lake-side paths, and accessible forest trails. The network offers options for both easy and moderate ability levels, with no difficult routes. The routes in Villenave-D'Ornon are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 30 reviews. More than 140 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Villenave-D'Ornon's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Villenave-d'Ornon?
A: There are 14 mountain bike trails available around Villenave-d'Ornon. These routes are generally categorized as easy to moderate, offering a good range for different skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Villenave-d'Ornon?
A: The terrain around Villenave-d'Ornon is diverse, featuring urban green spaces, tranquil lake-side paths, and accessible forest trails. You'll find winding paths through parks like Parc de Mussonville and Parc de l'Ermitage, well-maintained paths along Versein Lake, and more natural, unpaved sections in nearby forests such as Canéjan Forest. The routes are generally flat with gentle elevation changes.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes around Villenave-d'Ornon are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986742927/leognan-and-la-brede-forest-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Léognan and La Brède Forest Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate route that takes you through extensive forest areas. Another great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41565447" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cayac Priory – Moulin de Rouillac loop from Villenave d'Ornon&lt;/a&gt;, which explores a mix of green spaces and historical sites.

Q: What is the longest mountain bike route I can take from Villenave-d'Ornon?
A: The longest mountain bike route available is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41028279" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridges over the Bourde Stream – Moulin de la Moulette loop from Villenave d'Ornon&lt;/a&gt;, covering a distance of 51.8 km (32.2 miles). This moderate trail offers a comprehensive exploration of the local landscape.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Villenave-d'Ornon?
A: Villenave-d'Ornon offers several easy mountain bike routes that are suitable for families. The generally flat terrain and numerous parks with winding paths make for pleasant rides. While specific 'family-friendly' designations aren't always explicit, the 5 easy routes available are a good starting point for less experienced riders or families with children.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Villenave-d'Ornon?
A: The mountain bike routes in Villenave-d'Ornon are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 30 reviews. Reviewers often praise the varied terrain, the mix of urban green spaces and natural paths, and the accessibility of the trails for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or attractions to see along the mountain bike trails?
A: While riding, you might encounter various points of interest. The region is known for its beautiful parks like Parc de Mussonville and Parc de l'Ermitage. For historical sights, you could explore routes that pass near attractions such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4080325" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Château La Louvière&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6748658" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Château de La Brède&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41560751" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moulin de Rouillac – Green Lake loop from Villenave d'Ornon&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, leads through varied landscapes including lake areas.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Villenave-d'Ornon?
A: The region is suitable for cycling in any season, thanks to its varied landscapes including forests and parks. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, while summer can be enjoyable, especially on shaded forest paths. Even in winter, the trails can be a 'huge playground' for mountain biking, provided conditions are dry.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the Bourde Stream area?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41562504" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridges over the Bourde Stream – Moulin de Rouillac loop from Villenave d'Ornon&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 37.7 km (23.4 miles) route that specifically explores the area around the Bourde Stream, offering scenic views and a pleasant ride.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of mountain bike trails in Villenave-d'Ornon?
A: The mountain bike trails around Villenave-d'Ornon are categorized as easy or moderate. There are 5 easy routes, perfect for beginners or a relaxed ride, and 9 moderate routes for those looking for a bit more challenge. There are no difficult routes in this immediate area, making it accessible for a wide range of riders.

Q: Can I combine mountain biking with visiting other natural areas near Villenave-d'Ornon?
A: Absolutely. Villenave-d'Ornon is conveniently located near larger natural areas. The Landes de Gascogne Regional Natural Park, about 47 km away, offers extensive hiking and cycling paths. Closer options include Bois du Burck and Canéjan Forest, which provide more opportunities for off-road experiences and can be integrated into longer rides or separate excursions.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of the Boucle à partir de Villenave d'Ornon mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate. While some sections might be easy and paved, be prepared for unpaved and potentially difficult segments that require good fitness. The varied terrain includes nature trails, rural paths, and some dedicated cycle tracks.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking for this exact starting point isn't detailed, Villenave d'Ornon offers various public parking options. It's advisable to look for parking near the town center or residential areas that provide easy access to the local cycling network.

faqs.question: Is the Boucle à partir de Villenave d'Ornon trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is generally dog-friendly. However, as it traverses varied terrain including municipal roads and shared paths, it's essential to keep your dog on a leash, especially in populated areas or near wildlife habitats. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this mountain bike loop?
faqs.answer: The route is generally practicable all year round, especially sections like the 'Graves route'. However, for the most comfortable experience, spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and less chance of extreme weather. Summer can be hot, and some unpaved sections might be muddy after heavy rain in winter.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to ride the Boucle à partir de Villenave d'Ornon. Access to the public paths and trails in the area is generally free.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied. You'll encounter nature trails, dedicated cycle tracks, municipal roads, and rural paths. The route winds through green spaces, alongside streams, and into sections of the Landes forest. Some parts are unpaved and can be challenging, while others are smoother.

faqs.question: What interesting sights or features will I encounter along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers diverse scenery. You'll pass through sections of the Landes forest and potentially near the Graves vineyards. Highlights on this specific Komoot tour include a Singletrack Along Saucats Airfield, a chance to Splash through stream ford, and enjoyable Singletrack in the Pines.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with any other cycling paths or networks?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers small sections of the ReVE 7 - Bordeaux - Villenave d'Ornon (5%) and the EV3 France 40, Bordeaux - Hostens (4%), which is part of the longer EuroVelo 3 (La Scandibérique) network. You'll also encounter parts of the Chemin de la Ceste and the Coulée Verte.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or restaurants near the route?
faqs.answer: Villenave d'Ornon and the surrounding towns offer various amenities. While the route itself might pass through rural areas, you can find cafes, restaurants, and shops in the urban and suburban sections, particularly closer to Villenave d'Ornon itself. It's a good idea to plan your stops in advance.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its 'moderate' difficulty rating and varied terrain, including potentially difficult unpaved sections, this specific 46 km loop might be challenging for younger children or families seeking a very easy ride. However, shorter, easier, and mostly paved sections exist within the broader Villenave d'Ornon cycling network that are more family-friendly. Consider the fitness level of all riders before attempting the full loop.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this loop?
faqs.answer: For this moderate 46 km route, you should bring plenty of water, snacks, a basic bike repair kit (spare tube, pump, multi-tool), a helmet, appropriate cycling attire, and sun protection. A fully charged phone with the Komoot app for navigation is also highly recommended.
